summary: Make Conversation Agent model input cache-ready while preserving append-only transcript audit state.
rationale: The Conversation Agent now sends the model a stable registered prompt, latest summary, included recent transcript, advisory memory, runtime facts, and the current user message. The full transcript remains append-only for UI and audit, and compaction marks summarized messages as excluded from future model input in the same store transaction.
deletion_targets: raw user text used as the whole model prompt
conversation compaction split across summary and message-inclusion writes
advisory memory text interpolated without data-only JSON escaping
line_count_rationale: service.py is already a pre-existing oversized Conversation Agent orchestration module. This PR keeps the model input builder call, token accounting, runtime-facts path, and compaction transaction at the existing orchestration boundary so the cache-ready input contract is tested without introducing a speculative service split.
risks: Prompt-marker text inside user messages or memory must remain JSON data, not executable prompt structure.
Compaction must update summary, compaction row, and model_input_included flags atomically.
Runtime fact answers must use the same registered instructions as normal Conversation Agent turns.
